articles.title: Best Next Study: A Case of Customer Satifaction and Retention
articles.contents:
Contents
Load Required Packages
Load Data from BestNextStudy Package and Reorder Factor Levels
Calculating A-Efficiency and D-Efficieny




Load Required Packages

  suppressPackageStartupMessages({
    require(devtools)  #to load the package from Github
    require(dplyr)#for better manipulating data

  })
devtools::install_github("bestnextstudy/bestnextstudy")
library(bestnextstudy)

Load Data from BestNextStudy Package and Reorder Factor Levels

#customer_satisfaction_data is pre-built into the package
data = bestnextstudy::customer_satisfaction_data
###REORDER FACTOR LEVELS
data$Exchange <- factor(data$Exchange, levels = c("Goods", "Services", "Mixed"))
data$Market <- factor(data$Market, levels = c("B2C", "B2B", "Mixed"))
data$Location <- factor(data$Location, levels = c("North America", "Europe", "Asia"))
data$CS_Scale_Items <- factor(data$CS_Scale_Items, levels = c("Single Item", "Multiple Items"))
data$CS_Scale_Points <- factor(data$CS_Scale_Points, levels = c("5", "7", "10"))

Calculating A-Efficiency and D-Efficieny

variables =  c("Exchange", "Market", "Location", "CS_Scale_Items", "CS_Scale_Points", "Retention_Measure")
labels = c("Exchange", "Market", "Location", "CS_Scale_Items", "CS_Scale_Points", "Retention_Measure")
corrvar = "Corr"
##RUN
eff = bestnextstudy::efficiency(data = data, variables = variables, labels = labels, corrvar = corrvar)
eff
      N a_efficiency d_efficiency
[1,] 30     28.13088     53.44657

articles.title: BestNextStudy
articles.contents:
The R package BestNextStudy brings a friendly syntax to study
how well have extant studies collectively contributed to our understanding of the focal association being studies in a meta-analysis at a given point in time
how has our understanding of the focal association being studies in a meta-analysis evolved over time
what should be the optimal next study to further our understanding of the focal association being studies in a meta-analysis
The application page demonstrates the use of BestNextStudy package to understand the association between Customer Satisfaction and Retention using sample data from Mittal et. al (2022).
The package is available on GitHub and can be installed to your local computer by running the following lines of code.


# Installing `devtools` package if not alredy installed
if(!require(devtools)){
    install.packages("devtools")
    library(devtools)
}

#Installing `bestnextstudy` package
devtools::install_github("bestnextstudy/bestnextstudy")
library(bestnextstudy)
